storyInfo: The protest against the Rhine Lab observation station is dispersed. Victoria delivers a large shipment of aid supplies, but there seem to be ulterior motives behind the act of kindness.

attributes.content: There they are! The unclean who defiled Mount Karlan and the faith of Kjerag!
attributes.name: Shouting Villager

attributes.content: This iron house of foreigners has angered Kjeragandr!
attributes.name: Shouting Villager

attributes.content: O Kjeragandr on high, forgive our sins...
attributes.name: Male Villager

attributes.content: The sound of praying and sobbing, mixed with angry shouting.
The sounds weave into a rope, connecting tearful, fearful and angry faces, surrounding the newly-built observation station.
The steel door remains unmoving. A wizened voice cuts through the crowd.
'Remember that the wind wrapping the mountains does not come from nowhere.'
attributes.name: Old Woman

attributes.content: The wind continues to roar. The old woman walks across the snow to the tightly shut door, her voice clearly heard by the others.
'It is Her breath, sweeping down from the snow-white peaks.'
attributes.name: Old Woman

attributes.content: 'It is Her ever-vigilant watch, rising from the deepest valley to the highest cloud.'
attributes.name: Female Villager

attributes.content: 'It will drive out the miasma of evil, and the parasites that hide amongst the rocks.'
attributes.name: Villagers

attributes.content: 'It brings all the sounds back to Her ears, so that She will hear, and the mountains bright and clear—'
attributes.name: Villagers

attributes.content: Wait... is that door moving?
attributes.name: Female Villager

attributes.content: Yes! They're opening the door! Our protest worked!
attributes.name: Shouting Villager

attributes.content: Open up! Open up and hear our voice!
attributes.name: Shouting Villager

attributes.content: Listen to the true voice of Kjeragandr's people! Listen to the voice of Kjerag! You are not welcome here! You must leave!
attributes.name: Shouting Villager

attributes.content: Outsiders off Mount Karlan! Go back where you came from!
attributes.name: Female Villager

attributes.content: Outsiders off Mount Karlan!
attributes.name: Shouting Villager

attributes.content: Outsiders off Mount Karlan!
attributes.name: Shouting Villager

attributes.content: The cries grow louder and clearer. Someone stretches out and waves toward the sky.
Is it the result of sincere prayer? Or did the combined force of their invocations cause steel to tremble?
The door opens.
All breaths are abated in the silence.
That garb... are they from Clan Silverash?
attributes.name: Male Villager

attributes.content: The one behind them... the Edelweiss? Did he come to send these outsiders away?
attributes.name: Female Villager

attributes.content: He'd better. They were the ones who brought those people in, and they should be the ones to pick up the mess. But... something about his expression...
attributes.name: Male Villager

attributes.content: Clear out the protesters. Avoid casualties.
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: Put down your weapons! Back off!
attributes.name: Silverash Clan Guard
Female Villager
attributes.content: We're not backing off! Why are you telling us to go away? The ones who should go away are...

attributes.name: Female Villager
attributes.content: Keep your hands off me!
Edelweiss! Whose side are you on?!
attributes.name: Female Villager

attributes.content: Whose side do you think they're on? Their weapons and shields are pointed towards us... isn't it clear enough who his friends and enemies are?
attributes.name: Male Villager

attributes.content: A blizzard is coming.
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: You should leave before the road is completely blocked.
attributes.name: Gnosis
Gnosis
attributes.content: No need to worry about the dangers along the way. Clan Silverash's guards will ensure your safety and bring you back to...

attributes.name: Gnosis
attributes.content: Ugh!
Grandma, wait, don't—
attributes.name: Female Villager

attributes.content: The old woman forces her arm through the guards' ranks.
Her movements are swift and without hesitation. The cold snow splashes upon the Liberi's face.
May Kjeragandr forgive your sins.
attributes.name: Old Woman

attributes.content: Pity and resolute mercy congregate upon the wrinkled face.
Her cold finger touches Gnosis's forehead, leaving behind a simple curved line.
A Kjerag ritual, intended to drive the evil out of a child afflicted by fever.
May Her miracle drive out the miasma of evil, and the parasites that hide amongst the rocks from you...
attributes.name: Old Woman

attributes.content: Remember that the winds of Kjeragandr have visited upon your stubbornness before.
attributes.name: Old Woman

attributes.content: Now, you send Her away.
attributes.name: Old Woman

attributes.content: Go home, madam.
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: There is no change in Gnosis's expression as snowmelt flows down his face.
The winds on the mountains are harsh. Don't catch a cold.
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: Kjerag. The beautiful paradise, clean and pristine beneath the wind and snow, pious and pure under divine protection.
The faith of its people is firm as the mountains, never wavering... or perhaps, a single flake of snow is all it takes to begin its collapse...
'What do you think of the Catastrophe? Will it shake your faith in Kjeragandr?'
The faith of Kjerag isn't so weak! The people of Kjerag have never wavered, even before the toughest trial!
attributes.name: Kjerag Elder

attributes.content: I'll set out for Mount Karlan once the Catastrophe is cleaned up and my leg is good again.
attributes.name: Kjerag Elder

attributes.content: The sun of Kjerag will drive out all corruption and evil, and those who bathe in it need fear neither pain nor illness, nor the Stone Disease...
attributes.name: Kjerag Elder

attributes.content: Kjeragandr is great, Kjeragandr is kind. She will never abandon Kjerag. Everything will go back to the way it was, once we clean up the snow...
attributes.name: Kjerag Elder

attributes.content: Of course! It's all the fault of those Rhine Lab people!
attributes.name: Kjerag Girl

attributes.content: Everything started going bad when they came! Kjerag doesn't need no observation station! All we need is Kjeragandr's protection!
attributes.name: Kjerag Girl

attributes.content: Drive them out! Give me back my peaceful life... and my mom and dad...
attributes.name: Kjerag Girl

attributes.content: Is there anything I can help... wait, you're Victorian?
attributes.name: Kjerag Woman

attributes.content: Get your pitchforks! Who let this one into the village? Columbians and Victorians, is everybody making their homes on our turf now?
attributes.name: Kjerag Woman

attributes.content: Get lost! You're not welcome here!
attributes.name: Kjerag Woman

attributes.content: Phew... this will be tougher than I thought.
attributes.name: Victorian Reporter

attributes.content: Do you need some help?
attributes.name: Passing Girl

attributes.content: Hi... I'm fine, I'm just short on film. I don't suppose I'll be able to find any here...
attributes.name: Victorian Reporter

attributes.content: Let me see... you can get this model from the central mall in the Silverash domain.
attributes.name: Ensia

attributes.content: Nice pics. Are you a photographer from Victoria?
attributes.name: Ensia

attributes.content: A reporter, to be exact.
attributes.name: Victorian Reporter

attributes.content: Good timing. May I get an interview?
attributes.name: Victorian Reporter

attributes.content: Who? Me?
attributes.name: Ensia

attributes.content: How do you view the Catastrophe that just occurred in Kjerag? How do you view your faith, when Kjeragandr no longer protects Kjerag from Catastrophe?
attributes.name: Victorian Reporter

attributes.content: Ah, I thought I heard you talking about Kjeragandr and Catastrophes from over there...
attributes.name: Ensia

attributes.content: You want to know how Kjerag people think?
attributes.name: Ensia

attributes.content: Of course. We came to report on the true Kjerag. People in other lands know very little about it, and we want your voices to reach them.
attributes.name: Victorian Reporter

attributes.content: There's no need to be burdened by the weight of religion. Liberate yourself from the systemic oppression of rules and tradition, and tell me what you truly feel.
attributes.name: Victorian Reporter

attributes.content: Wow, I've never heard so many big words spoken in a single breath...
attributes.name: Ensia

attributes.content: My view on Catastrophes is simple... Catastrophes outside Kjerag are really bad, and it's the same for this Catastrophe in Kjerag.
attributes.name: Ensia

attributes.content: People suffer and get hurt everywhere. It makes me sad, and I wish these things never happened.
attributes.name: Ensia

attributes.content: As for 'faith'... may I ask a question? Does Victoria have a god too?
attributes.name: Ensia

attributes.content: Victoria...?
attributes.name: Victorian Reporter

attributes.content: Yes. I'm curious what the god of Victoria is like. Is He really petty? Does He get angry easily?
attributes.name: Ensia

attributes.content: You've been going around the village asking questions for days, but you don't talk about anything other than 'Kjeragandr is angry' and 'Kjeragandr has abandoned you.'
attributes.name: Ensia

attributes.content: So I thought, maybe 'faith' to you is something that's colder, harsher.
attributes.name: Ensia

attributes.content: A smile appears on the girl's face. Her cheeks rise, and her voice is light and spry.
I believe the god of Kjerag isn't like that.
attributes.name: Ensia

attributes.content: Kjeragandr is like the people of Kjerag, generous and kind.
attributes.name: Ensia

attributes.content: She doesn't get angry over cable cars and factories, or light bulbs and books. And, She won't throw a tantrum by causing a Catastrophe and taking lives.
attributes.name: Ensia

attributes.content: Kjeragandr isn't petty like that.
attributes.name: Ensia

attributes.content: Heave, ho! One more box, move over—
attributes.name: Stout Villager

attributes.content: Alright, put it down. You've got room on your truck. We got another fifteen barrels of burdenbeast milk wine? Can you bring it with you?
attributes.name: Newly Married Man

attributes.content: Fifteen barrels? Mm...
attributes.name: Stout Villager

attributes.content: You ought to keep it. Can't have a big bar like this and nothing for guests.
attributes.name: Stout Villager

attributes.content: The three great clans alone have donated plenty of stuff to the affected village, not to mention the local gentry. A few barrels of wine more or less won't make much difference.
attributes.name: Stout Villager

attributes.content: I don't like to hear that.
attributes.name: Newly Married Man

attributes.content: They can donate but I can't? Who are you to decide whether they need it or not? Which of us has actually seen a Catastrophe before?
attributes.name: Newly Married Man

attributes.content: But that's too much...
attributes.name: Stout Villager

attributes.content: It's not. I know you're short on wine.
attributes.name: Newly Married Man

attributes.content: Not only does wine keep people warm, it can also be used in medicine, and even to disinfect wounds when necessary.
attributes.name: Newly Married Man

attributes.content: If we had all this stuff back then... well, never mind.
attributes.name: Newly Married Man

attributes.content: Anyway, you're taking it, not one barrel less, or I'll take it as you saying that I'm not one of you and I don't belong to Kjerag!
attributes.name: Newly Married Man

attributes.content: Wait, wait, that's not what I meant!
attributes.name: Stout Villager

attributes.content: Then take it. Think of it as doing me a favor. You know how that father-in-law of mine is like.
attributes.name: Newly Married Man

attributes.content: He's endured hardship all his life, but he's never been stingy when it comes to sharing with hungry people... now move aside and let me put the cargo in.
attributes.name: Newly Married Man

attributes.content: Just don't get in any trouble and make him even angrier.
attributes.name: Stout Villager

attributes.content: I still remember how he huffed and puffed when you proposed to his daughter.
attributes.name: Stout Villager

attributes.content: 'I thought he was a nice guy who just wanted to help, turns out he was after my little girl Freya!' He said.
attributes.name: Stout Villager

attributes.content: He said it and he was still mad, so he pulled out his cane... hehe...
attributes.name: Stout Villager

attributes.content: What are you lot chatting so excitedly about?
attributes.name: Freya

attributes.content: Let's get to work. Dad and I just checked our stock. We still have about two truckloads of stuff, mostly dried meat and cheese, plus some butter and herbs.
attributes.name: Freya

attributes.content: Good thing I caught you before you left, Crete. Need to tell Karlan Trade to send another truck here.
attributes.name: Freya

attributes.content: Two trucks? You...
attributes.name: Stout Villager

attributes.content: I told you, Crete.
attributes.name: Lisburn

attributes.content: Fine, fine, suit yourself. You're the only newlyweds I know who'd rather give away their whole storehouse before taking any gifts.
attributes.name: Stout Villager

attributes.content: It's a Catastrophe, after all.
attributes.name: Freya

attributes.content: It's never happened to Kjerag. No one knows how bad it is.
attributes.name: Freya

attributes.content: But now is the time for us to work together and help each other... in Kjeragandr's name.
attributes.name: Freya

attributes.content: In Kjeragandr's name. We'll work together and pull through.
attributes.name: Stout Villager

attributes.content: Let's go back, Lisburn. The wind's getting stronger.
attributes.name: Freya

attributes.content: I can't help but have a feeling...
attributes.name: Lisburn

attributes.content: A feeling of what?
attributes.name: Freya

attributes.content: A strange feeling... that something's about to happen...
attributes.name: Lisburn

attributes.content: No, I must be overthinking it...
attributes.name: Lisburn

attributes.content: Your intuition is as sharp as always.
Wait... Harold? Is this real? Am I dreaming?
attributes.name: Lisburn

attributes.content: Well, that's certainly an honor.
attributes.name: Harold

attributes.content: You still remember my name. Looks like you haven't forgotten the time we fought alongside each other.
attributes.name: Harold

attributes.content: Please, stop making fun of me. What brings you here?
attributes.name: Lisburn

attributes.content: What else could it be?
attributes.name: Harold

attributes.content: We've heard there's a spot of trouble in Kjerag, so we came to offer some warmth, as a good neighbor should.
attributes.name: Harold

attributes.content: Wow... a whole truck of supplies...
attributes.name: Lisburn

attributes.content: A truck? Take another look.
attributes.name: Harold

attributes.content: Harold gestures behind himself, and Lisburn sees a long procession of vehicles bearing the Victorian crest, stretching as far as the eye can see.
Some warmth, you say?
attributes.name: Lisburn

attributes.content: Well, we're warmer than some other folk, to be sure.
attributes.name: Harold

attributes.content: I must emphasize our concerns regarding this matter, Mr. Silverash.
attributes.name: Columbian Ambassador

attributes.content: We consider Karlan Trade a reliable partner, and Kjerag a respectable nation.
attributes.name: Columbian Ambassador

attributes.content: We had hoped that the observation station would be the tip of the spear of our mutual understanding and cooperation.
attributes.name: Columbian Ambassador

attributes.content: I have to say, at the moment, we are disappointed.
attributes.name: Columbian Ambassador

attributes.content: I understand Your Excellency's concerns. Allow me to explain—
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: No, I don't need an explanation. We understand the unfortunate circumstances that have befallen Kjerag and we express our deepest sympathies.
attributes.name: Columbian Ambassador

attributes.content: But that is no reason for Kjerag mobs to attack innocent Columbian scientists.
attributes.name: Columbian Ambassador

attributes.content: We're seeing verbal abuse of our scientists, promoting hate and xenophobia—
attributes.name: Columbian Ambassador

attributes.content: We are outraged by Kjerag's contempt for and violation of the safety of other people.
attributes.name: Columbian Ambassador

attributes.content: Should your government continue to allow the situation to spiral out of control, the Columbian side will take action to ensure the safety of Rhine Lab's researchers by evacuating them, in accordance with our treaty.
attributes.name: Columbian Ambassador

attributes.content: Regarding the incident at the Rhine Lab observation station, Karlan Trade personnel have arrived on site, and are working to de-escalate the conflict, preventing the situation from further deteriorating.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: As I understand it, there has been no injury to person or property.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: Should we thank our lucky stars? Or that public sentiment has not yet reached a boiling point?
attributes.name: Columbian Ambassador

attributes.content: Should we continue to trust in Kjerag's ability to stabilize the situation? Or should that public outrage dissipate overnight?
attributes.name: Columbian Ambassador

attributes.content: The fireplace cracks, sending forth a wave of warmth, but there is no warmth on the faces in the room.
The Columbian ambassador adjusts her posture and raps the table with her finger, speaking in a calm tone.
We respect the professionalism, sincerity, and capability that Karlan Trade has demonstrated in the past.
attributes.name: Columbian Ambassador

attributes.content: It has allowed our cooperation and scientific collaboration to progress safely, up to this point.
attributes.name: Columbian Ambassador

attributes.content: Now, however, the Columbia's best and brightest are in danger on your land, danger that they never should have found themselves in.
attributes.name: Columbian Ambassador

attributes.content: Kjerag needs to provide a solution... or at least, demonstrate a will to resolve the situation.
attributes.name: Columbian Ambassador

attributes.content: Your Excellency cannot ask so much of a... developing country.
attributes.name: Victorian Ambassador

attributes.content: Considering Kjerag's current circumstances, I find Mr. Silverash's performance thus far quite impressive.
attributes.name: Victorian Ambassador

attributes.content: ...I see that Victoria shows great care for her neighbor.
attributes.name: Columbian Ambassador

attributes.content: But of course.
attributes.name: Victorian Ambassador

attributes.content: A kind neighbor should always be ready to lend a kind hand. Remember the prince of Victorian folklore, who gave his golden crown to a fowlbeast in the snow.
attributes.name: Victorian Ambassador

attributes.content: The man smiles with an expression of unnatural benevolence.
He draws out a piece of paper from his pocket, and unfolds it on the table.
Here is a checklist for the first catch of humanitarian assistance.
attributes.name: Victorian Ambassador

attributes.content: Food, blankets, medicine, spirits, sugar, salt, a team of doctors, and two veteran peacekeeping squads.
attributes.name: Victorian Ambassador

attributes.content: Exactly what Kjerag needs right now.
attributes.name: Victorian Ambassador

attributes.content: This is very generous. Kjerag appreciates your kindness.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: Not mine. This is the kindness of the Empire of Victoria.
attributes.name: Victorian Ambassador

attributes.content: Our prince had a heart of gold. He would give his prized sword in the same way he would offer gold and silver. It all depends on what you need, and what manner of threat you face.
attributes.name: Victorian Ambassador

attributes.content: ...Hmph.
attributes.name: Columbian Ambassador

attributes.content: There is no need to fear or worry. I'm sure you will make a decision that is satisfactory... to all three sides of us.
attributes.name: Victorian Ambassador

attributes.content: After all, survival is an art form in the Snow Realm... is it not, Mr. Silverash?
attributes.name: Victorian Ambassador

attributes.content: Gazes beckoning and questioning converge upon the piece of paper, and on the silent young man.
What are your thoughts, Mr. Silverash?
attributes.name: Columbian Ambassador

attributes.content: Enciodes looks down at the pushed piece of paper and picks it up.
Black and white, neat and clear. There is no deception; even the names of the squad leaders are listed on the paper, a perfect show of sincerity and pomposity.
I have no reason to refuse, do I?
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: As you said, this is exactly what Kjerag needs right now.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: The supplies will keep the people fed, and the doctors will help us win more battles against illness and death.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: As for trained soldiers... I'm sure I do not need to expound upon usefulness.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: To the question that was interrupted, Madam Ambassador...
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: Kjerag vowed to protect the safety of each and every researcher at the station when it was first established.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: That vow has not been broken. I'm not sure why you are so eager to reach the conclusion that Kjerag has lost control of the situation.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: The Vine-Bead Court and Karlan Trade will uphold our end of the bargain. May I ask you to continue to trust in Kjerag's ability to deal with our own problems?
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: As for whether the incident at the observation station was an accident, or instigated by those with ulterior motives... I believe that time will tell.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: Very well, Mr. Silverash. I look forward to your show of competence.
attributes.name: Columbian Ambassador

attributes.content: But I must remind you how important the project is to Columbia. We believe it to be of paramount significance to all Terra.
attributes.name: Columbian Ambassador

attributes.content: Therefore, the Columbian side will deploy any means necessary to protect our scientists and their findings. Any means necessary.
attributes.name: Columbian Ambassador

attributes.content: Indeed. I, too, believe that the scientific exploration taking place in Kjerag belongs to all of Terra.
attributes.name: Victorian Ambassador

attributes.content: The Empire of Victoria will do our part to maintain Kjerag's stability.
attributes.name: Victorian Ambassador

attributes.content: Of course...
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: I'm sure we're all of one mind on this matter.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: The sun shines in through the window, past the shoulder of the Columbian ambassador, and upon the chest of the Victorian ambassador.
He stands up from his chair, his back perfectly straight. The discussion seems to have come to a close.
Such a wise decision. It seems all of us have gotten what we wanted.
attributes.name: Victorian Ambassador

attributes.content: ...Phew.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: Is the meeting over?
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: How long have you been waiting?
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: Half an hour. I made good use of it going through four reports while I was waiting.
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: Take a seat. If you want a drink, help yourself.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: Thank you, but I've already helped myself to the tea that you kept on top of the shelf. It has been there for a little too long.
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: There's finally time to ask you. What's the situation at Rhine Lab?
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: They have no insights into the Catastrophe. As expected.
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: Our people demonstrated enough restraint. There weren't any real attempts at violence. Most satisfied themselves with reciting the Kjeragandr as they held hands.
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: But I noticed that there were a few people at the front who shouted the loudest, only to disappear into the crowd when they saw the soldiers.
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: Are you concerned?
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: I'm concerned that this may only be the first incident.
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: The observation station is not the only place where conflict is happening. There have been protests and rallies in the domains of the other two clans. Strikes at our factories as well—I'm dealing with it.
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: Someone understands that they can use this Catastrophe as a weapon. They know the power of the people's will.
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: A trick that you've deployed before. But the weapon is not in your hand this time.
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: Hm...
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: You skipped the part where someone threw snow in your face.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: ...Is that important?
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: It's the only thing that's unexpected.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: Did you expect Columbia and Victoria to sit you down for a game of good cop/bad cop?
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: Yes and no.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: The Columbians' attitude is aggressive, but at the end of the day, it's continued cooperation that they want. The most important thing, for them, is that the observation station delivers results.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: As for the Victorians... they're more anxious than we thought.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: I saw Harold's caravan. I'm sure he's wandering about the streets, telling neighborly stories with his 'old friends'.
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: His wandering should take him to Karlan Trade headquarters in another twenty minutes.
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: I suppose you've already signed and received the goods. And I don't suppose we had a choice.
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: The Paleroches and Browntails will find out soon. As for how they'll take it... let's just say you should be prepared for the Tri-Clan Council.
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: Getting cold feet?
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: I'm used to it.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: This nonchalant attitude of yours is why people hate you so much.
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: Thank you for the compliment.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: At the end of the day, however, none of us expected this Catastrophe to happen.
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: You can act as unflappable as you like, but don't tell me this disaster hasn't actually fazed you.
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: I don't think you're actually that thick.
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: ...I know.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: With risk, however, comes opportunity... it's the first Catastrophe that Kjerag has seen in a thousand years. Perhaps it's time for certain things that have lasted a thousand years to change.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: Enciodes looks out the window. It has started snowing again, covering the land of Kjerag in white.
The mountain winds continue to blow. They call it the breath of Kjeragandr, Her messenger.
It walks every inch of Kjerag, bringing all its sounds back to Her ears.
She will tell right from wrong, good from evil, friend from foe, piety from falsehood, medicine from poison, and mirth from tears.
She will pass judgment.
There's one other thing that you should know.
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: The avalanche caused by the Catastrophe destroyed the train station at the foot of the mountain. Strangely, and fortunately, there were fewer people inside the station than expected.
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: It was only in later interviews that we learned that someone had shouted about a bomb in the station, causing the crowd to flee.
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: Who?
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: We don't know, yet.
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: But the researcher who disappeared from Rhine Lab remains unaccounted for.
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: If they are one and the same, then she had not cleared the borders of Kjerag when the Catastrophe occurred. She should still be somewhere in the realm.
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: I see.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: You're the only one I can count on for this, Gnosis.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: Find this woman, without triggering a response from Rhine Lab.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: You're finally starting to look serious... should I ask why?
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: You'll learn soon enough.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: But ideally, one should not have to rely on trick plays in the game of chess.
attributes.name: Enciodes
